prompts.sparse:
Write testthat tests for this R function:

```r
siteInfo <- function(object) {
  if (!inherits(object, "commaData")) {
    stop("'object' must be a commaData object")
  }
  gr <- rowRanges(object)
  df <- data.frame(
    chrom = as.character(seqnames(gr)),
    position = start(gr),
    strand = as.character(strand(gr)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  mc <- mcols(gr)
  site_key <- paste0(
    df$chrom, ":", df$position, ":", df$strand, ":",
    mc$mod_type, ":", mc$motif
  )
  cbind(df, site_key = site_key, mc)
}
```

Just give me the test code.
prompts.detailed:
Write testthat tests for this exported R function in the comma Bioconductor package. The function reconstructs a flat data frame from a commaData object.

The test data object is `comma_example_data` (588 sites: 393 6mA, 195 5mC). The returned data frame has columns: chrom, position, strand, site_key, mod_type, motif, plus any other mcols columns. The site_key format is `chrom:position:strand:mod_type:motif`.

Write tests for: (1) input validation (errors on non-commaData), (2) return type (data.frame), (3) column presence, (4) site_key format correctness, (5) row count matches the object.

```r
siteInfo <- function(object) {
  if (!inherits(object, "commaData")) {
    stop("'object' must be a commaData object")
  }
  gr <- rowRanges(object)
  df <- data.frame(
    chrom = as.character(seqnames(gr)),
    position = start(gr),
    strand = as.character(strand(gr)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  mc <- mcols(gr)
  site_key <- paste0(
    df$chrom, ":", df$position, ":", df$strand, ":",
    mc$mod_type, ":", mc$motif
  )
  cbind(df, site_key = site_key, mc)
}
```

Just give me the test code, no explanation.

test_cases: contains expected columns (chrom, position, strand, site_key, mod_type, motif)
row count matches the commaData object
expected.scoring_notes: Must test input validation (expect_error), return type, column presence, and site_key format. Should use comma_example_data. Deduct for tests that don't actually test the contract.
